reasoning: Confirmed by code. ajax_gate() (class-wc-order-export-admin.php lines 530-538) dispatches to WC_Order_Export_Ajax::$method() when $_REQUEST['tab'] is absent or not in $this->tabs, and calls die() immediately after — the check_admin_referer() at line 546 is unreachable from this branch. The global handler class (class-wc-order-export-ajax.php) exposes two public ajax_* methods: ajax_run_one_job() has its own check_ajax_referer() guard and is protected; ajax_export_download_bulk_file() has NO nonce check at all (confirmed by phpcs:ignore NonceVerification comment at line 68-69), reads $_REQUEST['export_bulk_profile'] attacker-controlled, and triggers a full WooCommerce order export and download. The wp_ajax_order_exporter hook is logged-in-only, so reachability is correctly scoped to users with view_woocommerce_reports or export_woocommerce_orders capability. A cross-site request to wp-admin/admin-ajax.php?action=order_exporter&method=export_download_bulk_file with no tab parameter executes the export with no CSRF token required. WC_Order_Export_Ajax only uses WC_Order_Export_Ajax_Helpers trait, not WC_Order_Export_Admin_Tab_Abstract_Ajax_Export, so the check_nonce()-guarded tab methods are not reachable through this branch. No missed sanitizer or guard was found. The finding is structurally confirmed.
